name: Garlic Dill Pickles- Country Living
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Water (if the tap water in your home is hard or high in mineral content, use bottled water to avoid getting shriveled or dark pickles)
4 heads Fresh or dried dill seeds
recipeInstructions:
1. In a piece of double-thick cheesecloth, tie the pickling spices to form a small bag. In a stainless-steel 3-quart saucepan, heat the spice bag, vinegar, water, sugar, and salt until boiling. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer the vinegar mixture (brine) for 15 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, prepare two wide-mouth 1-quart canning jars and lids for processing. Halve the cucumbers lengthwise. Pack them lightly into hot jars; add 2 dill seed heads, 2 cloves garlic, and 2 red peppers to each jar.
3. Discard the spice bag from the brine. Ladle the simmering brine over the cucumbers, leaving 1/2 inch of space at the top of the jar. Keep the brine simmering while filling both jars. Place a small knife or metal spatula between the cucumbers and inside of the jar; move it around to release any air bubbles that may exist. Seal jars with lids.
4. Place jars on a rack in a 12-quart canner filled with boiling water (water should cover the tops of the jars by an inch); cover the canner and process the jars for 15 minutes. Cool jars; label and store in a cool, dark place for at least 2 months before serving. Store opened jars of pickles in the refrigerator.
